executive_summary.one_paragraph: HSPA5/GRP78 is a canonical ER-luminal chaperone that undergoes stress-induced translocation to the plasma membrane surface in multiple cancer contexts — AML (especially FLT3-ITD+), glioblastoma (mesenchymal subtype), brain tumor stem cells, breast, ovarian, and pancreatic cancers. Direct surface detection by BxxP-HRP biotinylation MS (leukemia lines) and surfaceome MS (HGG patient-derived cultures) confirms plasma-membrane accessibility in disease. Surface expression is absent or minimal in unstressed/normal cells. A concurrent secreted/EV-associated form and rare surface staining in primary tissue IHC are the principal accessibility risks.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: HSPA5/GRP78 surface expression is supported by two independent direct method types from multiple sources. First, cell-surface mass spectrometry using BxxP-HRP proximity biotinylation on intact, non-permeabilized leukemia cell lines (MV4-11, Molm13, RS4;11) detected HSPA5 at the plasma membrane (a1_evi_04, a1_evi_05, a1_evi_06; PMC12849903). Second, surfaceome MS analysis of adult and pediatric high-grade glioma patient-derived cultures directly identified GRP78 surface translocation (a1_evi_10; PMC12772127), with corroborating review note that other groups confirmed surface GRP78 by flow cytometry and Western blot on non-permeabilized cells (a1_evi_09). These represent independent sources using direct non-permeabilized surface detection. Contradictory signals exist: IHC in one clinical cohort found only rare focal surface staining (a1_evi_20), and EV/conditioned-medium data indicate a secreted/shed form (a1_evi_14, a1_evi_17, a1_evi_18, a1_evi_19). However, the direct surface biotinylation + surfaceome MS data from multiple independent studies and cell types outweigh these caveats, supporting direct_multi_method despite the conflicting IHC note.

expression_observations.context: AML cell lines MV4-11 and Molm13 — elevated/specific surface ER-associated proteins including HSPA5
Non-AML leukemia RS4;11 cell line — HSPA5 also detected on surface, contradicting AML-specific claim
Adult and pediatric high-grade glioma patient-derived cultures (aHGG and pHGG PDCs) — surface translocation of GRP78/HSPA5 identified by surfaceome analysis

antibodies.name: anti-HSPA5
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: High paralog identity with HSPA1A/B/8 (~63-65%); no KO validation mentioned.

expression_observations.context: Extracellular vesicle fractions (low- and medium-speed centrifugation pellets) from various cancer cell lines — HSPA5 enriched in large EV fractions

antibodies.name: anti-GRP78
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: High paralog identity with HSPA1A/B/8 (~63-65%); cross-reactivity not assessed.

expression_observations.context: Conditioned medium from cells overexpressing CS-TM536 — HSPA5 detected by immunoblot after IMAC purification of soluble TM; HSPA5 associates with secreted TM in extracellular compartment
Human plasma extracellular vesicles — HSPA5 classified as large EV marker, signal gradually depleted after 20 years of plasma storage
Clinical cohort tumor samples — only one case with few cells showing cell surface BiP/HSPA5 localization by IHC; surface expression rare or focal

antibodies.name: anti-GRP78
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Antibody biotinylated with Abcam kit and conjugated to streptavidin microbubbles for targeted ultrasound imaging; no clone or catalog specified.

expression_observations.context: Human macrophages — cell-surface-enriched protein fractions collected alongside whole-cell lysates and secreted proteins for MS; HSPA5 not explicitly named in this clip

non_surface_expression.context: Pediatric high-grade glioma (pHGG) tumor samples vs. non-neoplastic brain (NNB); HSPA5 mRNA unchanged between groups by RNA-seq
HGG patient-derived cultures; poor correlation between mRNA levels and surface protein presence for HSPA5 by transcriptomic/proteomic analysis
Clinical cohort tumor samples (unspecified cancer type); IHC showing only one case with few cells exhibiting surface BiP/HSPA5 localization

therapeutic_engagement.description: Multiple preclinical programs target cell-surface GRP78 (HSPA5) across cancer indications. CBT100, CBT200, and CBT300 are GRP78 inhibitors developed specifically to inhibit only cell-surface GRP78 in brain tumor cells (PMC13052156). Anti-GRP78 antibodies have been conjugated to streptavidin-microbubbles for targeted ultrasound imaging (PMC9954741). Anti-BiP antibodies are noted as being in pre-clinical trials (PMC9777260). Broader preclinical development of chemical or antibody drug candidates targeting surface HSPA5 is reported across leukemias and solid tumors (PMC12849903). No approved drug or confirmed clinical trial for surface-GRP78 targeting is documented in this ledger.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: HSPA5/GRP78 exists in both ER-resident and cell-surface forms, and also appears in secreted/EV-associated pools. The therapeutic programs documented here (CBT100/200/300, anti-GRP78 antibodies) are explicitly designed to engage the plasma-membrane-surface form on cancer cells, not ER-resident GRP78. Ledger evidence (a1_evi_03, a1_evi_21) confirms antibody and small-molecule targeting of extracellularly accessible GRP78. The secreted/EV forms (a1_evi_14, a1_evi_17, a1_evi_19) are distinct and not the intended therapeutic target.

contradicting_evidence.claim: IHC analysis of a clinical tumor cohort found that surface BiP/HSPA5 expression was nearly absent: only a single case showed even a few cells with cell-surface localization. Despite pre-clinical therapeutic interest in surface GRP78, this finding indicates that surface expression is rare or focal in primary tumor tissue rather than broadly present.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: Surface translocation of HSPA5/GRP78 is well-documented to be stress-context- and cell-type-dependent; the clinical cohort may not have represented the high-stress tumor microenvironments (e.g., hypoxia, glucose deprivation) that are known to drive ER-to-surface redistribution. Additionally, fixation-based IHC may under-detect dynamic surface pools compared to live-cell flow cytometry, and tumor heterogeneity could account for the focal single-case observation.

cell_states.descriptor: ER stress (including chronic oncogenic ER stress) drives translocation of HSPA5 from its canonical ER compartment to the plasma membrane; surface levels are low/absent in unstressed cells but increase with UPR magnitude.
Chronic oncogenic stress, particularly in FLT3-ITD+ AML subtypes, drives aberrant surface translocation of HSPA5, positioning it as a disease-state-specific surface neoantigen in AML.

Surface HSPA5 is induced across multiple tumor contexts (brain tumors, AML, breast, ovarian, pancreatic cancers), where it promotes aggressive growth, chemoresistance, and stem cell expansion; absent or rare in matched normal tissue.

dual_localization.condition: ER stress, oncogenic stress, or pathophysiological conditions (e.g., cancer, AML, brain tumors)

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Any stressed or pathophysiological cell state
accessibility_modulation.change: HSPA5/GRP78 is predominantly ER-resident under normal conditions but translocates to the plasma membrane surface under ER stress, oncogenic stress, or disease states. Baseline surface expression is absent or minimal; stressed/disease states drive surface accumulation.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface HSPA5 is accessible to extracellular binders only in stressed or disease contexts. In normal unstressed cells, the protein is sequestered in the ER lumen and inaccessible to antibodies or drugs applied extracellularly.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Unstressed cells with HSPA5 confined to the ER lumen
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Cells experiencing ER stress (including chronic ER stress in tumor contexts)
accessibility_modulation.change: ER stress triggers translocation of HSPA5 from the ER to the cell surface. Surface HSPA5 levels increase substantially; the protein is absent or undetectable on the plasma membrane without stress.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: ER stress converts HSPA5 from an intracellular target to a cell-surface-accessible antigen, enabling extracellular targeting with antibodies or drug conjugates specifically in stressed cells.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal / non-malignant hematopoietic cells (healthy PBMCs, non-leukemic lines)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: AML cells, particularly FLT3-ITD+ AML, under chronic oncogenic stress
accessibility_modulation.change: HSPA5 aberrantly localizes to the cell surface of AML blasts (e.g., MV4;11, Molm13) but is not prominently surface-displayed on healthy PBMCs. Chronic oncogenic stress drives this mislocalization, enriching HSPA5 in the AML surface proteome.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface HSPA5 is selectively accessible on AML blasts versus normal hematopoietic cells, providing a potential therapeutic window for antibody-based targeting in FLT3-ITD+ AML and related subtypes.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Brain tumor cells (glioma/glioblastoma, especially mesenchymal GBM subtype) under oncogenic/ER stress
accessibility_modulation.change: Surface GRP78/HSPA5 is induced in brain tumor cells as a stress-induced factor distinct from ER-resident GRP78. HSPA5 mRNA and protein are elevated in GBM versus normal brain, highest in the mesenchymal subtype. Surface HSPA5 stabilizes oncogenic receptor complexes (ROR1, Cripto, PD-L1).
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface HSPA5 is accessible on brain tumor cells and functionally engages extracellular receptor partners, making it a tractable surface target for therapeutic or diagnostic agents in GBM, particularly mesenchymal subtype.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal breast tissue or non-malignant breast epithelium
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Breast cancer cells (PR+ and triple-negative subtypes); primary breast tumor tissue
accessibility_modulation.change: Surface HSPA5/BiP is detected on breast cancer cell surfaces and has prognostic associations. However, in a primary patient cohort, membranous surface localization was extremely rare (only one case), suggesting surface HSPA5 is heterogeneous and infrequent in primary breast tumors.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface HSPA5 accessibility in breast cancer is variable and generally low in unselected primary tumors, limiting broad applicability of surface-targeting strategies; may be relevant in specific stressed or treatment-resistant subpopulations.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Ovarian cancer and pancreatic cancer primary tumor tissue
accessibility_modulation.change: Membranous (cell surface) HSPA5/GRP78 staining was detected by IHC in a subset of ovarian and pancreatic cancer specimens, indicating infrequent but detectable surface localization in these solid tumor types.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface HSPA5 is accessible to extracellular binders in a minority of ovarian and pancreatic cancer cases; patient selection or biomarker stratification would be needed to identify tumors with sufficient surface expression for therapeutic targeting.

surface_bind.attribution: © Marchand, Khakzad, Correia et al. — EPFL / Inria / Novo Nordisk

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: Surface HSPA5 acts as a co-stabilizer of ROR1, Cripto, and PD-L1 at the brain tumor cell surface, but its own surface translocation is driven autonomously by ER/oncogenic stress, not by obligate partnership with another receptor. No evidence in the ledger indicates a trafficking co-receptor requirement.

shed_form.mechanism: Enrichment in large EV / low- and medium-speed centrifugation pellets from cancer cell conditioned medium and human plasma; association with secreted TM in conditioned medium.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: Surface HSPA5 is detectable only in stressed/malignant cells; in primary patient tumor tissue IHC it was present in only one case with few cells (breast cancer cohort) and in a minority of ovarian/pancreatic cases — indicating expression is focal/heterogeneous rather than uniform across the tumor mass, restricting accessible subdomain to stressed subpopulations.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: HSPA5/GRP78 is a ~78 kDa soluble chaperone that, when surface-translocated, exposes its full ~630-residue lumenal/extracellular domain. Preclinical antibody and small-molecule engagement programs (CBT100/200/300, targeted microbubbles) confirm the extracellular portion is accessible to large macromolecular binders.

epitope_masking.rationale: Surface HSPA5 stabilizes receptor complexes (ROR1, Cripto, PD-L1) at the cell surface, and its extracellular face may be partially occluded by these partner interactions or by conformational changes associated with chaperone substrate binding. No direct epitope-mapping experiment in the ledger quantifies masking extent.

evidence.claim: Surface-bound GRP78 (HSPA5) is identified as a survival factor essential for tumor stem cell expansion in brain tumors, establishing direct surface expression of HSPA5 in a cancer stem cell context.

spans.quote: Surface-bound glucose-regulated protein 78 (GRP78) is a survival factor that is essential for tumor stem cell expansion.

evidence.claim: The paper explicitly distinguishes cell surface GRP78 (HSPA5) from ER-resident GRP78, characterizing surface GRP78 as a stress-induced form that promotes aggressive growth, chemoresistance, radioresistance, and stem cell expansion in brain tumors. This topology/localization claim is central to understanding HSPA5 surface accessibility as a stress-conditioned, non-canonical event distinct from canonical ER residence.

spans.quote: Cell surface GRP78, compared to endoplasmic reticulum-resident GRP78, has been defined as a stress-induced factor that can cause aggressive growth, chemoresistance, radioresistance, and stem cell expansion in brain tumors.

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement claim: GRP78 inhibitors CBT100, CBT200, and CBT300 were created to specifically inhibit only cell surface GRP78 (HSPA5) in brain tumor cells, representing a preclinical drug development program targeting the surface-exposed form of HSPA5. These compounds target the extracellular-accessible form of GRP78 at the plasma membrane rather than ER-resident GRP78.

spans.quote: To specifically inhibit only cell surface GRP78, we created and tested several GRP78 inhibitors, CBT100, CBT200, and CBT300.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) protein was detected on the cell surface of both AML (MV4-11, Molm13) and non-AML leukemia (RS4;11) cell lines by cell-surface proteomic analysis using BxxP-HRP biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry. The detection in RS4;11 (non-AML) cells contradicts a prior study claiming GRP78 as a unique AML cell-surface marker, but confirms surface presence of HSPA5 across leukemia types.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines MV4-11, Molm13; non-AML RS4;11

cell_context.disease_state: acute myeloid leukemia / acute lymphoblastic leukemia

spans.quote: Of note, a previous study has reported that GRP78 (HSPA5) protein was presented on the cell surface of AML cells; [ 48 ] however, we found this protein was also detected on the surface of RS4;11 cells (Figure S1d,e , Supporting Information), suggesting that GRP78 may not be the unique cell‐surface marker for AML cells.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation method using biotin-XX-phenol (BxxP) probe with HRP-catalyzed biotinylation in the presence of H2O2 on intact (non-permeabilized) cells. This proximity-labeling-style cell-surface protein labeling assay was applied to AML and control cell lines to identify surface-accessible proteins including HSPA5. Method family: surface proximity labeling / biotinylation; permeabilization: none (cell-surface only).

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines (MV4-11, Molm13, RS4;11) and PBMCs

spans.quote: Cell‐surface protein labeling assays were conducted following previously established methods. [ 38 , 39 , 40 , 41 , 42 ] These assays utilized the biotin‐XX‐phenol (BxxP) probe as the labeling agent, with horseradish peroxidase (HRP) catalyzing protein biotinylation in the presence of H 2 O 2 ( Figure 1 a ).

evidence.claim: AML cells (MV4-11 and Molm13) exhibit a distinct surface proteomic signature characterized by elevated or specific expression of ER-associated proteins including HSPA5, as detected by cell-surface MS using BxxP biotinylation. This supports stress-induced translocation of HSPA5 to the AML cell surface.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines MV4-11 and Molm13

spans.quote: Taken together, these findings suggest that AML cells, particularly MV4‐11 and Molm13, exhibit a distinct surface proteomic signature characterized by elevated or specific expression of ER‐associated proteins, implicating stress adaptation mechanisms in their biology.

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement: HSPA5 (GRP78) has been investigated as a potential therapeutic target on the cell surface of leukemias and solid tumors, with preclinical development of chemical or antibody drug candidates underway. This establishes therapeutic interest in cell-surface HSPA5 across multiple cancer indications.

spans.quote: [ 13 , 53 , 54 , 55 ] For instance, surface‐expressed P4HB has been identified in various malignant and normal cell types, including leukemic cells, vascular smooth muscle cells, and platelets, where it performs critical biological functions. [ 47 ] Similarly, HSPA5 has been investigated as a potential therapeutic target on the cell surface of some leukemias and solid tumors. [ 48 , 56 , 57 ] Preclinical development of chemical or antibody drug candidates targeting HSP90B1 is underway across multiple cancer types, such as breast and colorectal cancers.

evidence.claim: Review-level assertion that GRP78 (HSPA5) has been detected at the surface of cancer cells, listing it alongside other ER chaperones (CALR, CANX, GRP94, GRP170, HSP47, PDIA1) confirmed at the cancer cell surface. Evidence based on review of multiple prior studies.

spans.quote: Among the ER chaperone family, CALR, CANX, GRP78, GRP94, GRP170, HSP47, and PDIA1 ( Table ) have been detected at the surface of cancer cells (reviewed in refs , , ).

evidence.claim: GRP78 (HSPA5) has been confirmed at the surface of adult high-grade glioma (aHGG) cell lines by other research groups using Western blot or flow cytometry. However, the paper also notes that variability in surfaceome detection methods must be carefully considered, as different experimental techniques influence detection and quantification of cell surface proteins — constituting a methodological caveat for surface GRP78 claims.

spans.quote: Additionally, specific ER chaperones, such as GRP78 and PDIA1, have been confirmed at the surface of aHGG cell lines by other groups using different techniques ( e.g. , Western blot or flow cytometry). , Notably, variability in surfaceome detection methods must be carefully considered, as experimental techniques can influence the detection and quantification of cell surface proteins.

evidence.claim: Surface translocation of GRP78 (HSPA5) was identified in adult and pediatric high-grade glioma patient-derived cultures (PDCs) using surfaceome analysis. The paper reports direct surface detection of GRP78 alongside other ER chaperones in clinically relevant primary-derived tumor cultures.

spans.quote: Herein, we unraveled the expression landscape and identified the surface translocation of the ER chaperones GRP78, GRP94, PDIA1, CALR, CANX, HSP47, and GRP170 in aHGG and pHGG PDCs.

evidence.claim: The study found poor correlation between mRNA levels and surface protein presence for ER chaperones including GRP78 (HSPA5), confirming that RNA-level expression is not predictive of surface accessibility. This supports the designation of RNA-level HSPA5 data as non-surface evidence that should not be equated with surface expression.

spans.quote: Moreover, the transcriptomic and proteomic analyses confirmed a poor association between mRNA and the surface presence of these noncanonical membrane proteins.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 mRNA levels were unchanged in pediatric high-grade glioma (pHGG) versus non-neoplastic brain (NNB) samples by RNA-seq analysis, indicating that RNA expression does not reflect elevated surface presence of HSPA5 in this tumor type. This is a non-surface expression observation qualifying the surface data.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pHGG tumor samples and non-neoplastic brain

spans.quote: A significant overexpression of HSP90B1 ( p < 0.01), and CANX ( p < 0.0001) in pHGG compared to NNB was determined, while HSPA5 , P4HB , HYOU1 , and SERPINH1 remained unchanged ( Figure H,I,K–N).

evidence.claim: Antibody table identifying the HSPA5 antibody used: Cell Signaling Technology Cat# 3177, RRID: AB_2119845. This is a key methodological identifier for the AntibodyRef slot, enabling downstream methods builder to populate antibody clone/catalog/RRID details. No KO-validation of antibody specificity is mentioned in this clip.

spans.quote: REAGENT or RESOURCE SOURCE IDENTIFIER Antibodies HSPA5 antibody Cell Signaling Cat# 3177; RRID: AB_2119845 Cytochrome c antibody Cell Signaling Cat# 4280; RRID: AB_1039672 TSG101 antibody Santa Cruz Cat# sc-7964; RRID: AB_671473 CD9 antibody Santa Cruz Cat# sc-13118; RRID: AB_627245 Cav-1 antibody Santa Cruz Cat# sc-894; RRID: AB_631407 KRT18 antibody Abcam Cat# ab93741; RRID: AB_10563566 GM-130 antibody Santa Cruz Cat# sc-55591; RRID: AB_630303 HSP60 antibody Cell Signaling Cat# 4870S; RRID: AB_2085240 CD81 antibody Abcam Cat# ab79559

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (heat shock protein family A5, GRP78) was detected by immunoblotting in extracellular vesicle fractions (low- and medium-speed centrifugation pellets) from cancer cell lines. HSPA5 was enriched in large EV (low/medium-speed) fractions versus high-speed fractions, identifying it as a large EV-associated / secreted-form marker. This constitutes shed/secreted form evidence: HSPA5 is present in extracellular compartments released from cells.

spans.quote: Immunoblotting demonstrated enrichment of previously identified LO proteins, including from different cancer cell types, such as heat shock protein family A5 (HSPA5) and keratin 18 (KRT18) 7 , 13 , 26 in the low- and medium-speed fractions versus high-speed fractions.

evidence.claim: GRP78 (HSPA5) antibody identifier: Santa Cruz Biotechnology Cat# sc-166490. This is a methodological identifier for the AntibodyRef slot; no validation controls (KO/siRNA) are described in this clip.

spans.quote: Antibodies against β-actin (Cat#sc-47778), GAPDH (Cat#sc-365062), phospho-SAPK/JNK (Cat#sc-6254), c-Met (Cat#sc-8057), HSP27 (Cat#sc-13132), and GRP78 (Cat#sc-166490) were purchased from Santa Cruz Biotechnology (Santa Cruz, CA, USA).

evidence.claim: GRP78 (HSPA5) antibody identifier: Proteintech Cat# 11587-1-AP. Provides a second antibody catalog reference for HSPA5 detection methods, enabling cross-reactivity checks given the high paralog identity (~63-65%) with HSPA1A/B/8.

spans.quote: Antibodies against GRP78 (Cat#11587-1-AP) and CD44(Cat#15675-1-AP) were purchased from Proteintech (Rosemont, IL, USA).

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) was detected by immunoblot in the conditioned medium (CM) of cells overexpressing CS-TM536, indicating that HSPA5 is present in the extracellular space (secreted/shed form). This is shed/secreted form evidence: HSPA5 co-purified with soluble TM from CM by IMAC, suggesting it associates with secreted proteins in the extracellular environment. The secreted HSPA5 lacks the KDEL retention signal function in the extracellular context.

spans.quote: (A) His-tagged CS-TM 536 was overexpressed with inactive (−) or active (+) RHBDL2, soluble TM was purified by immobilized metal affinity chromatography from CM, and TM and HSPA5 were detected by immunoblot.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) was found to interact with soluble TM536 present in conditioned medium (CM), where sTM536 exists in the ER/Golgi lumen and is secreted. HSPA5 interaction with the intraluminal/secreted TM was barely detected after RHBDL2 cleavage removing the IMD domain. This clip establishes that HSPA5 is present in intraluminal / secreted contexts and can be found in conditioned medium — supporting a secreted/shed form risk designation.

spans.quote:
The proportion of intraluminal TM 536 was higher than that of TM WT ( Figure 5 E), implying that sTM 536 exists in the lumen of the ER and/or Golgi.
sTM 536 contained in the CM was found to be associated with the ER protein chaperone heat shock protein family A (Hsp70) member 5 (HSPA5) ( Figure 6 A), and this interaction was barely detected with RHBDL2-cleaved sTM 536 , which lacks IMD.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 was detected as a large EV marker in human plasma extracellular vesicles, with its signal gradually depleted after 20 years of plasma storage. HSPA5 is classified as a large EV marker, consistent with its presence in secreted/shed extracellular vesicle fractions from circulating cells — supporting a soluble/secreted form risk designation.

spans.quote: Large EV markers, like HSPA5, ITGB1, and ITGB6, were gradually depleted, while ACTN4, VAMP3, and HLA-B were irregularly identified after 20 years (Fig. 2 C).

evidence.claim: Anti-BiP (HSPA5) antibodies are noted to be in pre-clinical trials, citing surface GRP78 as the relevant therapeutic target. However, in the cohort studied, only one case showed few cells with cell surface localization by IHC, indicating that surface BiP/HSPA5 expression is rare or focal in this tissue type. This constitutes a contradictory/cautionary note: surface expression in the studied cohort was nearly absent despite pre-clinical therapeutic interest.

spans.quote: Although BiP cell surface expression is of specific relevance as there are currently anti-BiP antibodies in pre-clinical trials [ 68 ] in our cohort, we only found one case with few cells showing this subcellular localization.

evidence.claim: Anti-GRP78 antibody was biotinylated using an Abcam biotinylation kit and conjugated with streptavidin-containing microbubbles (Targeson, Inc.) for targeted ultrasound imaging. This establishes therapeutic/diagnostic engagement of cell-surface GRP78 (HSPA5) via antibody targeting, using an exogenous anti-GRP78 antibody (vendor: unspecified clone, Abcam kit). Method family: antibody-conjugated targeted imaging; the antibody targets extracellular-accessible GRP78 at the plasma membrane.

spans.quote: Briefly, anti-GRP78 antibodies were biotinylated using biotinylation kit (Abcam, Cambridge, MA, USA) and then conjugated with microbubbles containing streptavidin (Targeson, Inc., San Diego, CA, USA).

evidence.claim: Cell-surface-enriched protein fractions were collected from macrophages alongside whole-cell lysates and secreted proteins for mass spectrometry analysis. This methodological approach (fractionation into surface-enriched, whole-cell, and secreted compartments for MS) provides context for any HSPA5 detection in surface vs. secreted fractions, though HSPA5 is not named explicitly in this clip.

spans.quote: We collected whole-cell lysates, cell-surface-enriched proteins, and secreted proteins from macrophages for mass spectrometry (MS) analysis.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation method using membrane-impermeable Sulfo-NHS-SS-Biotin (0.25 mg/mL, 30 min, 4°C) on intact cells, quenched with 50 mM Tris (pH 8), then cells lysed. Method family: surface biotinylation; permeabilization: none (membrane-impermeable reagent used at 4°C ensures only surface-accessible proteins are labeled). This method was used in the study that detected secreted HSPA5 in conditioned medium.

spans.quote: Cells were incubated with the membrane-impermeable biotinylation reagent Sulfo-NHS-SS-Biotin (0.25 mg/mL; 30 minutes; 4°C), washed with Tris 50 mM (pH 8) to stop the reaction, and then lysed.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is predominantly localized to the endoplasmic reticulum under normal, non-stressed conditions. This is the canonical subcellular compartment, not the plasma membrane surface.

spans.quote: Heat shock protein family A (HSP70) member 5 ( HSPA5 ), also known as GRP78 or Bip, is predominantly localized to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) [ 13 ].

evidence.claim: Under various ER stress conditions, HSPA5 relocates from its canonical ER compartment to the cell surface, mitochondria, and nucleus, or forms protein complexes. This stress-induced translocation to the plasma membrane surface represents a key accessibility modulation event: baseline surface expression is absent/low in unstressed cells, but increases upon ER stress.

spans.quote: Under various ER stress, HSPA5 relocates to the cell surface, mitochondria, nucleus, or forms complexes with other proteins to perform its functions [ 14 , 15 ].

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) protein is detected on the cell surface of AML cell lines (MV4;11, Molm13) and also on non-AML RS4;11 (ALL) cells by surface labeling assay, indicating surface GRP78 is not exclusive to AML but is present across leukemia cell lines. This challenges the assumption that surface HSPA5 is a unique AML-specific marker.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines (MV4;11, Molm13) and non-AML ALL cell line (RS4;11)

cell_context.cell_type: acute myeloid leukemia / acute lymphoblastic leukemia

spans.quote: Of note, a previous study has reported that GRP78 (HSPA5) protein was presented on the cell surface of AML cells; [ 48 ] however, we found this protein was also detected on the surface of RS4;11 cells (Figure S1d,e , Supporting Information), suggesting that GRP78 may not be the unique cell‐surface marker for AML cells.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) has been investigated as a potential therapeutic target on the cell surface of leukemias (AML) and solid tumors; surface expression in these malignant contexts positions it as a disease-state-induced surface antigen with clinical relevance across multiple cancer types.

spans.quote: [ 13 , 53 , 54 , 55 ] For instance, surface‐expressed P4HB has been identified in various malignant and normal cell types, including leukemic cells, vascular smooth muscle cells, and platelets, where it performs critical biological functions. [ 47 ] Similarly, HSPA5 has been investigated as a potential therapeutic target on the cell surface of some leukemias and solid tumors. [ 48 , 56 , 57 ] Preclinical development of chemical or antibody drug candidates targeting HSP90B1 is underway across multiple cancer types, such as breast and colorectal cancers.

evidence.claim: ER chaperone proteins including HSPA5 that are normally confined to the ER aberrantly localize to the cell surface under specific pathophysiological conditions. This establishes the principle that surface HSPA5 is a context-dependent, pathophysiology-induced surface localization event rather than a constitutive surface presence.

spans.quote: Notably, we discovered that certain ER chaperone proteins, typically confined to the ER, aberrantly localize to the cell surface under specific pathophysiological conditions.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 mislocalization to the cell surface is strongly linked to chronic oncogenic stress, particularly in FLT3-ITD+ AML subtypes. This cell-state (FLT3-ITD+ oncogenic stress) drives aberrant surface HSPA5 presentation, providing a molecular basis for disease-state-specific surface accessibility modulation in AML.

spans.quote: This mislocalization was strongly linked to chronic oncogenic stress in several AML subtypes, at least including the FLT3‐ITD + AML subtype, positioning these chaperones as promising diagnostic markers and therapeutic targets.

evidence.claim: Integrative scRNA-seq and cell-surface proteomics in AML reveal that ER chaperone proteins translocate to the cell surface upon chronic ER stress. This establishes that surface HSPA5 is a stress-induced neoantigen presentation event, with chronic ER stress as the modulating state that drives surface translocation in AML cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines (MV4;11, Molm13) and primary AML patient cells

spans.quote: Through integrative analyses of single-cell RNA-seq, cell-surface proteomes, and cellular biology, ER chaperone proteins (e.g., HSP90B1 and P4HB) are identified as potential neoantigens that translocate to the cell surface upon chronic ER stress.

evidence.claim: Surface GRP78 (HSPA5) is defined as a stress-induced factor that is distinct from ER-resident GRP78. In brain tumors, surface GRP78 expression promotes aggressive growth, chemoresistance, radioresistance, and stem cell expansion. This establishes a clear dichotomy between the canonical ER-luminal HSPA5 (constitutive, non-surface) and the stress-induced surface-translocated HSPA5 (disease-state-specific, therapeutically relevant).

cell_context.cell_type: brain tumor cells / brain tumor stem cells

spans.quote: Cell surface GRP78, compared to endoplasmic reticulum-resident GRP78, has been defined as a stress-induced factor that can cause aggressive growth, chemoresistance, radioresistance, and stem cell expansion in brain tumors.

evidence.claim: Surface GRP78 (HSPA5) expressed on brain tumor cell surfaces binds and stabilizes transmembrane tyrosine kinase ROR1, oncofetal factor Cripto, and immune checkpoint PD-L1. This demonstrates that surface HSPA5 in brain tumor cells engages functionally relevant receptor complexes at the extracellular surface, with implications for tumor immunology and therapeutic targeting.

spans.quote: Herein, our data show that surface GRP78 binds and stabilizes a novel transmembrane tyrosine kinase called receptor tyrosine kinase-like orphan receptor-1, an oncofetal factor called Cripto, and a checkpoint protein called programed death-ligand 1 in brain tumor cells.

evidence.claim: Surface-bound GRP78 (HSPA5) is a survival factor essential for tumor stem cell expansion in brain tumors. This positions surface HSPA5 as a functionally important surface molecule in the brain tumor stem cell compartment, distinguishable from the canonical ER-resident role.

spans.quote: Surface-bound glucose-regulated protein 78 (GRP78) is a survival factor that is essential for tumor stem cell expansion.

evidence.claim: BiP (HSPA5) surface expression has been observed on breast cancer cell surfaces. Its prognostic significance is subtype-dependent: surface expression associates with good prognosis and chemotherapy response in PR+ breast cancers and in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), indicating that surface HSPA5 is present across breast cancer subtypes with distinct clinical implications.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: breast cancer tumor tissue (PR+ and TNBC subtypes)

spans.quote: BiP has been described in the cell surface of breast cancer cells; however, its prognostic value remains controversial: cell surface expression was associated with good prognosis and response to chemotherapy in PR+ breast cancers [ 12 ] and triple-negative breast cancer [ 13 , 66 ].

evidence.claim: In a breast cancer patient cohort, BiP (HSPA5) surface localization was extremely rare — only one case with few cells showing cell surface localization was identified. This suggests that surface HSPA5 is an infrequent, likely stress-induced event in primary breast tumor tissue, not a broadly expressed surface marker across breast cancer patients.

spans.quote: Although BiP cell surface expression is of specific relevance as there are currently anti-BiP antibodies in pre-clinical trials [ 68 ] in our cohort, we only found one case with few cells showing this subcellular localization.

evidence.claim: HSPA5 mRNA expression is elevated in GBM tumor tissues compared to normal brain tissue, with the highest expression observed in the mesenchymal (MES) subtype of GBM. This RNA-level data from TCGA establishes disease-context upregulation in brain tumor tissue relative to normal tissue.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: GBM tumor tissue vs normal brain (TCGA and Gulou cohorts)

spans.quote: Compared to normal tissue, HSPA5 and HSPA6 expression levels were elevated in GBM tissues, especially in MES subtype (Figs. 1C–F and S 1A, B ).

evidence.claim: IHC analysis confirms that HSPA5 protein expression is highest in the mesenchymal (MES) subtype of GBM. This provides protein-level validation in primary tumor tissue of the RNA-level finding, establishing HSPA5 as a high-expression marker specifically in mesenchymal GBM.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: GBM primary tumor tissue, MES subtype

spans.quote: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) analysis further confirmed that HSPA5 expression was highest in MES subtype (Figs. 1G and S 1C ).

evidence.claim: HSPA5 protein expression is unchanged between pediatric high-grade glioma (pHGG) and normal/non-neoplastic brain (NNB) tissue in a surface proteomic analysis. Unlike other ER chaperones (HSP90B1, CANX) which are significantly overexpressed in pHGG, HSPA5 does not show differential expression at the protein level in this pediatric brain tumor context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pediatric high-grade glioma (pHGG) tissue vs normal non-neoplastic brain (NNB)

spans.quote: A significant overexpression of HSP90B1 ( p < 0.01), and CANX ( p < 0.0001) in pHGG compared to NNB was determined, while HSPA5 , P4HB , HYOU1 , and SERPINH1 remained unchanged ( Figure H,I,K–N).

evidence.claim: AML cell lines (MV4;11 and Molm13) exhibit a distinct surface proteomic signature with elevated or specific expression of ER-associated proteins including HSPA5 relative to non-AML and healthy PBMC controls, implicating stress adaptation (chronic ER stress) as the driver of aberrant ER chaperone surface translocation in AML cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: AML cell lines MV4-11 and Molm13 vs RS4;11 (ALL) and healthy PBMCs

spans.quote: Taken together, these findings suggest that AML cells, particularly MV4‐11 and Molm13, exhibit a distinct surface proteomic signature characterized by elevated or specific expression of ER‐associated proteins, implicating stress adaptation mechanisms in their biology.

evidence.claim: Membranous (cell surface) staining for HSPA5/GRP78 was observed in a few cases of ovarian and pancreatic cancers by IHC, indicating that surface-facing protein is detectable at the plasma membrane in a subset of these tumor types. Surface expression appears infrequent rather than ubiquitous in these solid tumor histologies.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: ovarian cancer and pancreatic cancer primary tumor tissue

spans.quote: Membranous staining was observed in a few cases of ovarian and pancreatic cancers (Data not shown).

evidence.claim: HSPA5 (GRP78) is enriched in low- and medium-speed extracellular vesicle/large oncosome (LO) fractions from multiple cancer cell types, as detected by immunoblotting. This establishes HSPA5 as a component of cancer cell-derived large oncosomes across different tumor cell types.

spans.quote: Immunoblotting demonstrated enrichment of previously identified LO proteins, including from different cancer cell types, such as heat shock protein family A5 (HSPA5) and keratin 18 (KRT18) 7 , 13 , 26 in the low- and medium-speed fractions versus high-speed fractions.

evidence.claim: Gene ontology analysis of the AML surface proteome reveals significant enrichment of 'response to ER stress' pathway, encompassing specific chaperones including HSPA5, HSP90B1, P4HB, CANX, and CALR. This links HSPA5 surface presence in AML cells to an ER stress response gene signature.

spans.quote: These analyses demonstrated a significant enrichment of GO terms related to “response to ER stress,” which encompassed specific chaperones, including HSP90B1, HSPA5, P4HB, CANX, and CALR (Figure 3h–j ).

confidence_reasoning: A1 grades this direct_multi_method (BxxP-HRP biotinylation MS + surfaceome MS in independent cancer contexts), which is strong. However, three factors cap confidence at moderate: (1) A2 documents high state-dependence — surface expression is absent in unstressed/normal cells and stress/disease-state-induced, meaning the surface form is conditional, not constitutive; (2) primary tissue IHC shows near-absent surface staining in one clinical cohort (a1_evi_20, a2_evi_12), a direct contradiction to cell-line-based positive data; (3) antibodies used lack KO-validation (a1_evi_13, a1_evi_15, a1_evi_16), and the concurrent EV/secreted pool (a1_evi_14, a1_evi_17, a1_evi_19) introduces a decoy-form risk. The surface form is real but highly context-restricted, which merits moderate rather than high confidence.
